name: Competency Framework
description: Core, leadership, and functional competencies for Federal Civil Service officers

subcategories.name: Numerical & Mathematical Reasoning
subcategories.description: Applied numerical reasoning and quantitative judgement.

questions.question: If a civil servant earns ₦150,000 monthly and receives a 15% allowance, what is the total monthly income
A Ministry budgeted ₦2,000,000 for a project but spent ₦1,850,000. What is the percentage savings
A Department has 120 staff. If 25% are on leave, how many are at work
questions.explanation: 25% of 120 is 30, so 120 minus 30 leaves 90 staff at work.

questions.question: An officer received ₦45,000 as housing allowance which is 20% of basic salary. What is the basic salary
The price of a file increased from ₦200 to ₦260. What is the percentage increase
questions.explanation: Increase = ₦60. % Increase = (60 ÷ 200) × 100 = 30%. Correction: Correct answer is 30%.

questions.question: A directorate has a budget of ₦5,000,000. If 40% is allocated to personnel costs, how much is left for other expenses
If an officer retires after 35 years of service, and pension is calculated as 80% of final salary ₦250,000, what is the monthly pension
questions.explanation: 80% of ₦250,000 is ₦200,000, so that is the monthly pension shown by the correct option.

questions.question: What is ⅔ of 360
questions.explanation: Two-thirds of 360 is 240, so the correct answer is the option with that value.

questions.question: An officer is entitled to ₦120,000 annual leave grant. How much is this per month
questions.explanation: ₦120,000 spread across 12 months gives ₦10,000 per month.

questions.question: If 60% of 500 staff are female, how many are male
questions.explanation: 60% of 500 = 300 females. Male staff = 500 - 300 = 200.

questions.question: A project was estimated at ₦4,500,000 but cost ₦4,725,000. What is the percentage cost overrun
If ⅗ of a Ministry’s budget goes to salaries, and the total budget is ₦15,000,000, how much goes to salaries
questions.explanation: Three-fifths of ₦15,000,000 is ₦9,000,000, so that is the amount that goes to salaries.

questions.question: What is the simple interest on ₦100,000 at 10% per annum for 3 years
If the cost of 12 pens is ₦600, what is the cost of 30 pens
An officer receives ₦75,000 transport allowance, which is 25% of basic salary. What is the basic salary
If a file is processed in 6 hours by 3 officers, how many officers are needed to finish it in 2 hours
questions.explanation: Work is inversely proportional to time. Officers = 3 × (6 ÷ 2) = 9.

questions.question: What is the mean of the following salaries: ₦120,000, ₦130,000, ₦150,000, ₦100,000, ₦200,000
The ratio of male to female staff is 3:2. If there are 90 male staff, how many female staff are there
questions.explanation: 3 parts = 90 → 1 part = 30. Female = 2 parts = 60.

questions.question: An officer’s gross salary is ₦300,000. If deductions are 20%, what is the net salary
If the exchange rate is ₦750 to $1, how much is $200 in naira
questions.explanation: Multiply 200 by 750 to get 150,000, so $200 at ₦750 to $1 is ₦150,000.

questions.question: If a civil servant earns ₦120,000 monthly, what is the annual salary
questions.explanation: A monthly salary of ₦120,000 becomes ₦1,440,000 in a full year.

questions.question: If monthly salary is ₦160,000, there are 20 working days and 8 working hours per day, what is the overtime hourly rate at 1.5×
A project cost fell from ₦500,000 to ₦425,000. What is the percentage decrease
A budget is split in the ratio 3:5 between Unit A and Unit B. If total = ₦800,000, how much does Unit B receive
An item costs ₦50,000. VAT at 7.5% is added. What is the total price including VAT
An allowance is 12% of a basic salary of ₦250,000. What is the allowance amount
Find the mean of ₦10,000, ₦20,000 and ₦30,000.
A machine cost ₦1,000,000 with salvage value ₦100,000 and useful life 5 years. Straight-line depreciation per year is:
Officer A completes a task in 6 hours, Officer B in 8 hours. Working together, how long will they take (hours and minutes)
What is 5% of ₦2,000,000
questions.explanation: Five percent means 5 per 100, so 5% of ₦2,000,000 is ₦100,000.

questions.question: A salary of ₦200,000 is increased by 10% and then a 5% performance bonus is paid on the new salary. What is the bonus amount
A store had 500 units; 40% were used. How many units remain
A town of 1,000 people grows at 2% per annum. After 2 years the population is approximately:
What is the median of the set: 12, 15, 20, 22, 30
questions.explanation: Ordered list already; the middle (3rd) value is 20 → median = 20.

questions.question: A loan of ₦600,000 is to be repaid in 6 equal monthly installments. How much is each installment
questions.explanation: ₦600,000 divided into 6 equal installments gives ₦100,000 per installment.

questions.question: An item priced at ₦10,000 is offered at a 12% discount. What is the discounted price
If an item costs ₦1,000 and inflation is 5% for one year, what is the new price
A budget of ₦1,200,000 is split 60:40 between Director A and Director B. How much does Director A receive
In a batch of 5 items 2 are defective. What is the probability (as a percentage) that a randomly selected item is defective
questions.explanation: 2 out of 5 items are defective, so the probability is 2/5, which is 40%.

questions.question: Convert 150 minutes into hours and minutes.
questions.explanation: 150 minutes ÷ 60 = 2 remainder 30 → 2 hours 30 minutes.

questions.question: If a civil servant is entitled to 30 working days annual leave, how many leave days accrue per month (assume equal monthly accrual)
questions.explanation: 30 days ÷ 12 months = 2.5 days per month.

questions.question: An officer earns ₦500,000. With 18% tax, what is the net pay
Three projects cost ₦240,000, ₦360,000 and ₦600,000. What fraction of the total cost is the smallest project
questions.explanation: The total cost is ₦1,200,000 and the smallest project is ₦240,000, which is one-fifth of the total.

questions.question: Find the mode of the dataset: 12, 15, 15, 20, 22, 15, 30.
questions.explanation: The mode is the value that appears most often, and 15 occurs more than any other number in the list.

questions.question: A director’s performance score is weighted: 40% for targets (score 80) and 60% for behaviour (score 90). What is the weighted score
questions.explanation: Forty percent of 80 is 32 and sixty percent of 90 is 54, so the weighted score is 86.

questions.question: Overtime is paid at 2× hourly rate. If monthly salary ₦120,000, 22 working days, 8 hours/day, what is 3 hours overtime payment
If managers:staff ratio is 1:12 and there are 6 managers, what is total staff including managers
questions.explanation: Staff per manager = 12. Staff = 6×12 = 72. Total including managers = 72 + 6 = 78.

questions.question: Compute compound growth: ₦100,000 at 5% per annum for 2 years (compound annually).
What is the range of the dataset: 45, 60, 75, 90, 100
questions.explanation: Range = max − min = 100 − 45 = 55. Correct option: A (55.).

questions.question: An item has two successive discounts 10% then 5% on ₦20,000. What is the final price
Convert the fraction 7/20 to a percentage.
questions.explanation: 7 ÷ 20 = 0.35 = 35%. (Correction: 7/20 = 0.35 = 35% — correct index should be 1.).

questions.question: Solve: x + 3x = 800, what is x
A budget of ₦1,000,000 is cut by 12.5% then increased by 20%. What is the final amount
If an allowance of ₦90,000 represents 30% of basic salary, what is the basic salary
questions.explanation: If ₦90,000 is 30% of the basic salary, then the full salary is ₦300,000.

questions.question: Seven officers share ₦840,000 equally. How much does each receive
questions.explanation: ₦840,000 shared equally by 7 officers gives ₦120,000 each.

questions.question: A vehicle consumes 9 litres per day. For 22 working days, how many litres are needed
questions.explanation: 9 litres per day for 22 working days gives 198 litres in total.

questions.question: Exchange rate moves from ₦760/$ to ₦800/$. What is the percentage change (to 2 d.p.)
If training cost ₦450,000 and 15 participants share equally, how much per participant
questions.explanation: ₦450,000 divided by 15 participants gives ₦30,000 per participant.

questions.question: A staff strength increases from 240 to 300. What is the percentage increase
questions.explanation: The increase is 60 over a base of 240, so the percentage increase is 25%.

questions.question: A procurement lot of 120 units costs ₦72,000. What is unit cost
questions.explanation: ₦72,000 divided by 120 units gives a unit cost of ₦600.

questions.question: Which option best demonstrates good governance in numerical and mathematical reasoning tasks?
options: Approved numerical procedures and complete records.
questions.explanation: Approved numerical procedures and complete records strengthen compliance, consistency, and accountability in numerical and mathematical reasoning.

questions.question: For effective numerical & mathematical reasoning, what is the most appropriate approach to ensure numerical & mathematical reasoning compliance
options: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
questions.explanation: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in numerical & mathematical reasoning.

questions.question: Which choice best reflects sound risk management in numerical and mathematical reasoning work?
options: Early risk identification and prompt escalation.
questions.explanation: Early identification of control gaps and prompt escalation of material exceptions is the safest way to manage risk.

questions.question: When handling numerical & mathematical reasoning, which choice reflects proper numerical reasoning standards
options: Use quantitative evidence to support administrative decisions.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
questions.explanation: Use quantitative evidence to support administrative decisions. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in numerical & mathematical reasoning.

questions.question: Which practice should a responsible officer prioritize to sustain verbal reasoning in Numerical & Mathematical Reasoning
options: Interpret official text accurately before action.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
questions.explanation: Interpret official text accurately before action. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in numerical & mathematical reasoning.

questions.question: In the context of Numerical & Mathematical Reasoning, which action best demonstrates analytical judgement
options: Evaluate alternatives using logic and evidence.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
questions.explanation: Evaluate alternatives using logic and evidence. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in numerical & mathematical reasoning.

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "the best problem-solving approach in numerical and mathematical reasoning" according to official standards?
questions.explanation: Breaking complex issues into testable, actionable parts makes the reasoning easier to check and resolve.

questions.question: Which option most strongly aligns with good public-service practice on communication clarity within Numerical & Mathematical Reasoning
options: Present conclusions clearly with defensible rationale.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
questions.explanation: Present conclusions clearly with defensible rationale. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in numerical & mathematical reasoning.

questions.question: Which option best reflects proper documented procedure standards in numerical and mathematical reasoning work?
questions.explanation: Following documented procedure and keeping complete records makes the work traceable and reliable.

questions.question: Which practice should a responsible officer prioritize to sustain compliance assurance in Numerical & Mathematical Reasoning
options: Apply approved rules consistently and escalate exceptions.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
questions.explanation: Apply approved rules consistently and escalate exceptions. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in numerical & mathematical reasoning.

questions.question: Which option best demonstrates public accountability in numerical and mathematical reasoning work?
questions.explanation: Providing traceable decisions with evidence-based justification allows the work to be reviewed and defended.

questions.question: For effective numerical & mathematical reasoning, what is the most appropriate approach to ensure service integrity
options: Avoid conflicts of interest and disclose relevant constraints.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
questions.explanation: Avoid conflicts of interest and disclose relevant constraints. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in numerical & mathematical reasoning.

questions.question: Which option best supports risk control in numerical and mathematical reasoning work?
options: Early risk identification and documented mitigation.
questions.explanation: Identifying risk early, applying controls, and documenting mitigation reduces exposure and keeps the process controlled.

questions.question: When handling numerical & mathematical reasoning, which choice reflects proper decision transparency standards
options: Use clear criteria and communicate decisions promptly.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
questions.explanation: Use clear criteria and communicate decisions promptly. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in numerical & mathematical reasoning.

questions.question: Which practice best supports operational discipline in numerical and mathematical reasoning work?
questions.explanation: Following approved workflows and verifying outputs before closure keeps the work disciplined and accurate.

questions.question: Under the Public Procurement Act 2007, at what funding threshold does the Act apply to an entity that receives money for procurement from the Federation Share of the Consolidated Revenue Fund?
questions.explanation: The Act applies to entities that derive at least 35% of funds appropriated for any procurement from the Federation Share of the Consolidated Revenue Fund.

questions.question: What happens to an officer's National Housing Fund contributions when the officer exits the service?
options: The officer can access the contributions within one month of retirement.
The officer must apply directly to the National Housing Fund.
The officer must wait for a circular from the Head of Service.
The officer must provide a death certificate.
questions.explanation: Rule 021004 states that officers who exit the Service must access their National Housing Fund contributions within one month of retirement.

questions.question: Which body monitors the accruals to and disbursement of revenue from the Federation Account?
options: The Revenue Mobilization Allocation and Fiscal Commission.
questions.explanation: The Revenue Mobilization Allocation and Fiscal Commission monitors the accruals to and disbursement of revenue from the Federation Account.

questions.question: which role projects estimates of oil and non-oil revenues in consultation with revenue generating agencies?
options: The Budget Office of the Federation
questions.explanation: The Budget Office of the Federation prepares revenue estimates in consultation with revenue-generating agencies.

questions.question: What role may the Permanent Secretary perform in relation to other government bodies?
options: Clear a memorandum for funds with the Ministry of Finance before it goes to the Executive Council of the Federation.
Control the National Planning Commission directly.
questions.explanation: A Permanent Secretary may need to clear a memorandum involving funds with the Ministry of Finance before it is submitted to the Executive Council of the Federation.

questions.question: Who is the chief adviser to the Minister in a Ministry?
options: The Director of Finance and Accounts.
The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
questions.explanation: Within a ministry, the Permanent Secretary serves as the chief adviser to the Minister on administrative and policy implementation matters.

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "the role of the National Council on Establishments" according to official standards?
options: To set the salary for all government workers.
To manage the budget of the Federal Government.
To hire and fire civil servants.
To facilitate inter-governmental consultations on establishment matters and avoid unnecessary disparities in conditions of service.
questions.explanation: The National Council on Establishments facilitates inter-governmental consultations on establishment matters and helps avoid unnecessary disparities in the general conditions of service in the Civil Service.

questions.question: Who are public servants expected to study and imbibe the rules, instructions, guidelines, and extant circulars?
options: Only the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
Only new entrants into the Service.
questions.explanation: The preamble states that public servants are expected to study and imbibe the rules together with their associated and complementary instructions, guidelines, and extant circulars.

questions.question: What does the term ?Permanent Secretary? mean?
options: The Chief Executive Officer of a Parastatal.
The officer in charge of the day-to-day administration in a ministry or extra-ministerial office.
The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
questions.explanation: Rule 010106 defines Permanent Secretary as the officer in charge of the day-to-day administration in a ministry or extra-ministerial office.

questions.question: Who forms part of the membership of the Junior Staff Committee and Senior Staff Committee in parastatals?
options: A representative of the Department of Human Resource Management and an observer from the Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation and the Permanent Secretary.
The Board of Directors of the parastatal.
The Chief Executive Officer and the Financial Officer.
questions.explanation: Rule 020305 states that the membership of the JSC and SSC in parastatals includes a representative of the Department of Human Resource Management and an observer from the Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.

questions.question: Which office is responsible for paying the salary of an officer on secondment?
questions.explanation: The MDA to which the officer is seconded is responsible for paying the salary, while the parent MDA retains higher-level oversight.

questions.question: which office is accountable for posting and redeploying all senior staff?
options: The Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office a
The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (The Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office a). The rules state that all senior staff can be posted and/or redeployed to new posts in accordance with the approved Employee Mobility Policy52.

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "the overall goal of the pension scheme outlined in Chapter 19" according to official standards?
options: To provide short-term loans to employees.
To secure proper administration and accounting of pension contributions.
questions.explanation: Chapter 19 focuses on the administration of the pension scheme and the responsibilities of the relevant entities, showing that its goal is proper management of pension contributions.

questions.question: According to Section 2 of the Finance (Control and Management) Act 1958, what does ?Public Finance? include?
options: The Public Revenue of the Federation and any moneys held in an official capacity by a public service officer or government agent.
Any moneys held by a private citizen.
Only the Public Revenue of the Federation.
questions.explanation: Public Finance includes the Public Revenue of the Federation and any money held in an official capacity by a public service officer or government agent.

questions.question: Which of these is a source of revenue for the Consolidated Revenue Fund?
options: Proceeds from domestic consumption of crude oil.
Proceeds of tax under the pay-as-you-earn system from police personnel.
questions.explanation: Sources of revenue for the Consolidated Revenue Fund include the share from the Federation Account.

questions.question: choose the best answer for: The Development Fund is primarily meant for what type of projects?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Capital projects). This Fund is meant for capital projects. It is established by the 1999 Constitution.

questions.question: Which role is the Accounting Officer for a Ministry?
options: The Director of Finance and Accounts
The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
questions.explanation: The Permanent Secretary is the Ministry's Accounting Officer and is responsible for the ministry's finances and records.

questions.question: How does the 1999 Constitution define the Civil Service of the Federation?
options: As service in a civil capacity as staff of the Office of the President, Vice-President, a Ministry, or a department of the Government of the Federation.
As service in the National Assembly.
As service in all government agencies and parastatals without distinction.
questions.explanation: The 1999 Constitution defines the Civil Service of the Federation as service in a civil capacity in the Office of the President, the Vice-President, a Ministry, or a department of the Government of the Federation.

questions.question: Which office is the Accounting Officer of a Ministry?
options: The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation
The Director of Finance and Accounts.
questions.explanation: The Permanent Secretary is the Accounting Officer of a Ministry because that office is responsible for the ministry's funds and administration.

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "the chief role of the Auditor-General for the Federation" according to official standards?
options: To control the release of funds to MDAs.
To manage all government bank accounts.
To check and report on government expenditure and public accounts.
questions.explanation: The Auditor-General audits public expenditure and reports on public accounts, so that is the chief role of the office.

questions.question: Which activity should not be done during official working time?
questions.explanation: Civil servants are expected to give their best to official duties and should not use official working time for personal errands or private business.

questions.question: When must a public officer submit a declaration of assets and liabilities?
options: At the beginning, every four years, and at the end of tenure.
questions.explanation: A public officer must submit a declaration at the beginning of service, every four years, and at the end of the term of office.

subcategories.name: Verbal & Analytical Reasoning
subcategories.description: Verbal analysis, logical comprehension, and critical reasoning.

questions.question: What are the three main categories of competencies in the Federal Civil Service
questions.explanation: The Federal Civil Service competency framework groups competencies into Core, Leadership, and Functional categories.

questions.question: Choose the word that is most similar in meaning to 'prerogative
questions.explanation: 'Prerogative' refers to a special right or privilege exclusive to a person or group. The closest synonym here is 'Right'.

questions.question: Which of the following is the opposite of 'transparent'
questions.explanation: The opposite of transparent (clear, see-through) is opaque (not allowing light to pass through).

questions.question: Which word correctly completes the sentence: 'The officer was praised for his ______ in handling the matter'
questions.explanation: Tact means diplomacy or sensitivity, so it fits praise for handling a matter well.

questions.question: Select the pair with the same relationship: 'Doctor: Hospital':: 'Teacher: '
questions.explanation: The analogy is profession to workplace: a doctor works in a hospital, and a teacher works in a school.

questions.question: Which word is most opposite in meaning to 'benevolent'
questions.explanation: Ambiguous means unclear or open to more than one meaning, so uncertain is the closest match.

questions.question: Identify the correctly spelt word.
questions.explanation: Accommodate is the accepted spelling, so the other spellings are incorrect variants of the same word.

questions.question: What is the plural of 'crisis'
questions.explanation: Crisis becomes crises in the plural because words ending in -is often change to -es.

questions.question: Find the odd one out: Dog, Cat, Cow, Chair.
questions.explanation: Chair is the odd one out because it is furniture, while dog, cat, and cow are animals.

questions.question: Which word is most similar in meaning to 'expedite'
questions.explanation: Expedite means to speed up or hasten, so the correct answer is the word with that meaning.

questions.question: Choose the correct preposition: 'He insisted ___ his right to appeal.'
questions.explanation: Insist on is the fixed verb-preposition combination used in standard English, so on is the correct choice.

questions.question: Select the correctly punctuated sentence
options: The Permanent Secretary said 'I will attend tomorrow's meeting.'.
The Permanent Secretary said, 'I will attend the meeting.'.
The Permanent Secretary said, I will attend the meeting promptly.'.
The Permanent Secretary, said I will attend tomorrow's briefing.
questions.explanation: The correct punctuation is: The Permanent Secretary said, 'I will attend the meeting.'.

questions.question: Which of the following is the antonym of 'diligent'
questions.explanation: Careless is the opposite of diligent because diligent means careful, hard-working, and attentive.

questions.question: Which word best completes the sentence: 'The committee reached a unanimous ______ on the issue.
questions.explanation: Unanimous means fully agreed by everyone, so it is the option that shows complete agreement.

questions.question: Select the word that is opposite in meaning to 'scarce
questions.explanation: Scarce means in short supply, so the opposite is the word that means plentiful.

questions.question: Find the odd one out: Pen, Pencil, Paper, Book.
questions.explanation: Book is the odd one out because it is reading material, while the others are writing-related items.

questions.question: Choose the synonym for 'meticulous
questions.explanation: Meticulous means very careful and precise, so the closest word is the one with that meaning.

questions.question: Which indirect-speech change is correct for "I am ready"?
questions.explanation: In indirect speech, the present tense "am" backshifts to "was" after the reporting verb in the past tense.

questions.question: Which pair is correctly matched as synonyms: 'Expand: '
questions.explanation: Expand means increase or enlarge, so increase is the synonym that matches the question.

questions.question: Fill in the blank: 'She is very good ___ mathematics.'
questions.explanation: Good at is the standard collocation, so mathematics correctly follows the preposition at in this sentence.

questions.question: Select the correctly spelt word
questions.explanation: Conscientious is the correctly spelt form; the other options miss or rearrange letters.

questions.question: Choose the word that is most opposite in meaning to 'expand
questions.explanation: Expand means grow or increase, so the opposite is the word that means contract or shrink.

questions.question: What is the synonym of 'ambiguous'
questions.explanation: Ambiguous means unclear or uncertain, so the closest synonym is the option with that meaning.

questions.question: Which of the following completes the proverb: 'A stitch in time saves ____'
questions.explanation: A stitch in time saves nine is the established proverb, so nine completes it correctly.

questions.question: Which of these is the antonym of 'rigid'
questions.explanation: Rigid means stiff or inflexible, so the antonym is the word that means flexible.

questions.question: Which of these words is synonymous with 'impartial'
questions.explanation: Neutral is the closest synonym because impartial means unbiased or not taking sides.

questions.question: Which word best completes the sentence: 'The officer was known for his strict ______ to rules.
questions.explanation: The fixed phrase is strict adherence to rules, so adherence is the correct completion.

questions.question: Select the odd one out: Apple, Mango, Banana, Chair
questions.explanation: Chair is the odd one out because apple, mango, and banana are fruits, while chair is a piece of furniture.

questions.question: Which of the following is a synonym for 'obsolete'
questions.explanation: Outdated is the closest synonym because obsolete means no longer in use.

questions.question: Which word correctly completes the sentence: 'The manager gave a detailed ______ of the incident.'
questions.explanation: Account fits because it means a report or description of an incident, which matches the sentence meaning.

questions.question: Find the word most opposite in meaning to 'optimistic'.
questions.explanation: Pessimistic is the antonym of optimistic because it means expecting the worst rather than expecting a good result.

questions.question: What is the synonym of 'vivid'
questions.explanation: Vivid means bright, clear, or striking, so the closest synonym is the word with that meaning.

questions.question: Choose the correct indirect speech: She said, 'I will help you.
options: She said that she will help me.
She said that she would help me.
questions.explanation: In indirect speech, 'will' changes to 'would': She said that she would help me.

questions.question: Which of these is the antonym of 'generous'
questions.explanation: Generous means kind and giving, so the antonym is the word that means selfish or mean.

questions.question: Which of the following words is closest in meaning to 'lucid'
questions.explanation: Lucid means clear and easy to understand, so the closest synonym is the word with that meaning.

questions.question: Choose the correct completion: 'The committee was divided ______ the proposal.
questions.explanation: Divided on is the standard collocation used when people disagree about a proposal.

questions.question: Which proverb is correctly completed: 'Too many cooks spoil the ____'
questions.explanation: Too many cooks spoil the broth is the established proverb, so broth completes it correctly.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a synonym for 'happy'
questions.explanation: Miserable is not a synonym of happy; it is its opposite, which makes it the correct choice here.

questions.question: Choose the word that best completes: 'The new system will help to ______ efficiency.
questions.explanation: Improve efficiency is the intended meaning, so improve is the word that best completes the sentence.

questions.question: Select the word closest in meaning to 'resilient
questions.explanation: Resilient means strong and able to recover quickly, so the closest synonym is the word with that meaning.

questions.question: Which of these words is the antonym of 'complex'?
questions.explanation: Simple is the opposite of complex because it means not complicated, so it gives the best antonym here.

questions.question: Which of the following is a synonym for 'commence'?
questions.explanation: Begin is the direct synonym of commence, so it best matches the meaning of the original word.

questions.question: Choose the correctly spelt word.
questions.explanation: Definitely is the only correct spelling; the other forms are common misspellings that omit or rearrange letters.

questions.question: Which of these words is closest in meaning to 'benevolent'
questions.explanation: Benevolent means kind or well-meaning, so the closest synonym is the word with that meaning.

questions.question: What is the antonym of 'artificial'
questions.explanation: Artificial means not natural, so the antonym is the word that means natural.

questions.question: Choose the word that best completes: 'The new rules will ______ corruption in the service.
questions.explanation: Curb means reduce or restrain, so it fits the sentence about limiting corruption.

questions.question: Find the odd one out: Book, Pen, Teacher, Pencil.
questions.explanation: Teacher is the odd one out because the other items are stationery or writing tools.

questions.question: Which of the following is closest in meaning to 'candid'
questions.explanation: Candid means honest and open, so the closest synonym is the word with that meaning.

questions.question: Which of these is the antonym of 'expand'
questions.explanation: Expand means grow larger, so the antonym is the word that means shrink.

questions.question: Which word correctly completes: 'The officer was praised for his ______ in handling the crisis.
questions.explanation: Tact means diplomacy and sensitivity, so it fits praise for handling a crisis well.

questions.question: Choose the synonym of 'brief'.
questions.explanation: Short is the synonym of brief because both words mean lasting a little time or using few words.

questions.question: Which proverb is correctly completed: 'The pen is mightier than the ____'
questions.explanation: The pen is mightier than the sword is the established proverb, so sword completes it correctly.

questions.question: What is the antonym of 'prosperity'
questions.explanation: Prosperity means success or wealth, so the antonym is the word that means poverty.

questions.question: Which of these words is synonymous with 'fragile'
questions.explanation: Fragile means delicate or easily broken, so the synonym is the word with that meaning.

questions.question: Fill in the blank: "She is interested ___ music."
questions.explanation: The adjective "interested" is conventionally followed by the preposition "in," so the correct expression is "interested in music" rather than "on," "at," or "for."

questions.question: Which indirect-speech change is correct for "I am going to Abuja tomorrow"?
questions.explanation: In indirect speech, "am going" backshifts to "was going" and "tomorrow" becomes "the next day".

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "the antonym of 'rapid'" according to official standards?
questions.explanation: Slow is the opposite of rapid because rapid means fast, so slow gives the best antonym.

questions.question: Which of these words is closest in meaning to 'precise'
questions.explanation: Precise means exact and accurate, so the closest synonym is the word with that meaning.

questions.question: Which word best completes: 'The Head of Service issued a strong ______ on discipline.'
questions.explanation: Directive fits because it is a formal instruction, which matches a strong official order on discipline.

questions.question: Find the odd one out: Chair, Table, Bench, Bread.
questions.explanation: Bread is the odd one out because the other items are furniture, while bread is food.

questions.question: Which option best demonstrates good governance in verbal and analytical reasoning tasks?
questions.explanation: Approved procedures and complete records support consistency, compliance, and accountability in reasoning tasks.

questions.question: For effective verbal & analytical reasoning, what is the most appropriate approach to ensure verbal & analytical reasoning compliance
options: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
questions.explanation: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in verbal & analytical reasoning.

questions.question: Which choice best reflects sound risk management in verbal and analytical reasoning work?
options: Early risk identification and documented mitigation.
questions.explanation: Early risk identification and documented mitigation is the safest way to manage risk.

questions.question: When handling verbal & analytical reasoning, which choice reflects proper numerical reasoning standards
options: Use quantitative evidence to support administrative decisions.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
questions.explanation: Use quantitative evidence to support administrative decisions. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in verbal & analytical reasoning.

questions.question: Which practice should a responsible officer prioritize to sustain verbal reasoning in Verbal & Analytical Reasoning
options: Interpret official text accurately before action.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
questions.explanation: Interpret official text accurately before action. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in verbal & analytical reasoning.

questions.question: In the context of Verbal & Analytical Reasoning, which action best demonstrates analytical judgement
options: Evaluate alternatives using logic and evidence.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
questions.explanation: Evaluate alternatives using logic and evidence. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in verbal & analytical reasoning.

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "the best problem-solving approach in verbal and analytical reasoning" according to official standards?
questions.explanation: Breaking complex issues into testable, actionable parts makes the reasoning easier to check and resolve.

questions.question: Which option most strongly aligns with good public-service practice on communication clarity within Verbal & Analytical Reasoning
options: Present conclusions clearly with defensible rationale.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
questions.explanation: Present conclusions clearly with defensible rationale. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in verbal & analytical reasoning.

questions.question: Which option best reflects proper documented procedure standards in verbal and analytical reasoning work?
questions.explanation: Following documented procedure and keeping complete records makes the work traceable and reliable.

questions.question: Which practice should a responsible officer prioritize to sustain compliance assurance in Verbal & Analytical Reasoning
options: Apply approved rules consistently and escalate exceptions.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
questions.explanation: Apply approved rules consistently and escalate exceptions. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in verbal & analytical reasoning.

questions.question: Which option best demonstrates public accountability in verbal and analytical reasoning work?
questions.explanation: Providing traceable decisions with evidence-based justification allows the work to be reviewed and defended.

questions.question: For effective verbal & analytical reasoning, what is the most appropriate approach to ensure service integrity
options: Avoid conflicts of interest and disclose relevant constraints.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
questions.explanation: Avoid conflicts of interest and disclose relevant constraints. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in verbal & analytical reasoning.

questions.question: Which option best supports risk control in verbal and analytical reasoning work?
options: Early risk identification and documented mitigation.
questions.explanation: Identifying risk early, applying controls, and documenting mitigation reduces exposure and keeps the process controlled.

questions.question: When handling verbal & analytical reasoning, which choice reflects proper decision transparency standards
options: Use clear criteria and communicate decisions promptly.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
questions.explanation: Use clear criteria and communicate decisions promptly. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in verbal & analytical reasoning.

questions.question: Which practice best supports operational discipline in verbal and analytical reasoning work?
questions.explanation: Following approved workflows and verifying outputs before closure keeps the work disciplined and accurate.

questions.question: which option aligns with the meaning of the legal framework demand for processing short-term engagements?
options: A circular from the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation
The negotiation, drafting, and execution of contracts of engagement by the parties.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (The negotiation, drafting, and execution of contracts of engagement by the parties.). Rule 020421 states that due processes are followed in the "negotiation, drafting, and execution of contracts of engagement by the parties on all the short-term appointments." Part 7: Transfers and Secondments

questions.question: What must a Revenue Collector do immediately after receiving money?
options: Enter the receipt in the cash book without delay.
Keep the money in a safe until the end of the week.
Issue a verbal receipt and collect a written one later.
Hand it over to the Sub-Accounting Officer.
questions.explanation: Financial Regulation 209(i) requires every Revenue Collector to keep a cash book and enter all receipts without delay on the day of receipt.

questions.question: What must be kept in each strong-room or reserve cash safe for deposited cash, stamps, receipt/licence books, and other articles?
questions.explanation: Financial Regulation 1121 requires registers to be kept in each strong-room or reserve cash safe for all cash, stamps, receipt or licence books, and other deposited articles.

questions.question: What must be obtained when receipt and licence books are handed over from one officer to another?
questions.explanation: Financial Regulation 1226 states that when receipt and licence books are handed over from one officer to another, a handing-over certificate must be obtained.

questions.question: which role is in charge for keeping the up-to-date personal log of every officer?
options: The Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (The respective Ministry/Extra-Ministerial Office). Rule 020107 mandates that Permanent Secretaries/Heads of Extra-Ministerial Offices must keep an up-to-date log of every officer under their control.

questions.question: Which option best defines volunteerism under the rules?
options: Offering services for a short-term contract.
Offering one?s time or talent for charitable, educational, or other worthwhile activities, especially in one?s community, without remuneration.
Offering one's time or talent for a fee.
questions.explanation: Rule 020416 defines volunteerism as offering one?s time or talent for charitable, educational, or other worthwhile activities, especially in one?s community, without remuneration.

questions.question: Who is accountable for ensuring that approved fees are paid to invigilators and examiners?
options: The Permanent Secretary of the MDA.
The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
questions.explanation: The Permanent Secretary is accountable for the overall supervision of the examinations, including the disbursement of fees to invigilators and examiners.

questions.question: If a disbursement is made on account for a contract, what amount may be claimed?
options: The amount available in the vote.
An arbitrary percentage of the contract.
The cost of the work certified to have been performed.
questions.explanation: Financial Regulation 412 states that, in the case of payment on account, no money may be claimed other than the cost of the work certified to have been performed.

questions.question: What must support a withdrawal from a deposit?
options: The original receipt issued when the money was placed on deposit.
A handwritten note from the depositor.
A verbal confirmation from the Accounting Officer.
A photocopy of the original receipt.
questions.explanation: Financial Regulation 1306 states that withdrawals from deposit must be supported by the original receipt issued when the money was placed on deposit.

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "the ultimate aim of a due-process workflow policy designed to eliminate waste" according to official standards?
options: To prolong the duration of projects.
questions.explanation: The policy is aimed at eliminating waste so that public spending delivers value for money.

questions.question: In a Public Accounts Committee hearing, what is the role of the Accountant-General of the Federation?
options: To represent all MDAs and answer for them.
To provide clarification on accounting procedures and financial regulations.
questions.explanation: The Accountant-General attends the committee's sittings and provides clarification on accounting procedures, treasury circulars, and financial regulations.

questions.question: When should file notes be made?
questions.explanation: File notes should be made when verbal discussions occur so the record is complete, accurate, and traceable.

questions.question: Why are file notes made after verbal discussions in government administration?
options: To keep the record complete, accurate, and traceable.
To avoid writing any formal record.
questions.explanation: File notes are made so that government records remain complete and accountable after verbal discussions.

questions.question: How is a cheque or bank draft that is returned to the sender, redirected, or remitted to another station recorded?
options: By making an appropriate entry in the Paper Money Register.
By giving only verbal notice to the sender.
By recording it in the General Ledger.
By returning it without making any record.
questions.explanation: Financial Regulation 233(iii) requires an appropriate entry in the Paper Money Register, with reference to the covering memorandum or remittance voucher.

questions.question: which role can effect an alteration to the classification of a voucher?
options: The Head of Finance and Accounts. □
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (The officer controlling expenditure.). Financial Regulation 421 states: "An alteration to the classification of a voucher must only be effected by the officer controlling expenditure who will amend his Vote Book accordingly."

questions.question: Who is responsible for providing adequate accounting systems and controls in ministries and extra-ministerial offices?
options: The Head of Finance and Accounts.
questions.explanation: Financial Regulation 106 states that the Accountant-General of the Federation is responsible for providing adequate accounting systems and controls in ministries, extra-ministerial offices, and other arms of government.

questions.question: What should be done if an alteration to the amount of a voucher becomes necessary?
options: A new voucher must be prepared.
The alteration can be made and stamped ?Corrected?.
The alteration can be initiated by the certifying officer.
The Head of Finance and Accounts can approve the alteration.
questions.explanation: Financial Regulation 610 states that alterations to the amount of a voucher are not allowed, and a new voucher must be prepared when necessary.
